item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Every part of this is entirely predictable right from the description, and the characters involved are far too generic and shallow to support the article. For this article to be stronger, it would either need excellent characterisation, or a more unique focus. For example, extra development on the final addendum with an exploration into how exactly this anomaly affected a Foundation site could possibly have led to a better read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not super amazing but definitely good… with one major issue that NEEDS to be addressed: this anomaly appears ANYWHERE and is seemingly common enough to test. This seems like the anomaly extends way too far than it has any right to for the purpose of the lore. A good alternative would be to have it only trigger in one or a specific quantity of locations.
Aside from this, worth my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very enjoyable! I like how there's a bit of a hint that this might connect multiple alternate dimensions similar to our own together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's good, but the tone must be fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what if someone escaped from the anomaly and was carrying a journal? Just a thought.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is it feasible to deploy a reality anchor at every site? If it were, I would expect it to be standard procedure even before this SCP was ever discovered.

I am ready to +1, as I adore the premise—I'm creeped out nicely by the idea of getting lost in variable locations, and it's a recurring theme in some dreams. Also really like the exploration log.

If the one weak point about the reality anchors can be addressed you'll have my wholehearted upvote. Possibly some limitation to where or why it can appear, that makes at least attempting containment plausible in a manner besides "let's anchor reality at every site with our magic reality boxes."

You've got a couple breaks from clinical tone, but nothing I haven't been guilty of myself. Very interesting, similar to some other SCPs like the Infinite Ikea, but original enough to stand out. One might say, familiar… yet different :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Given the anomaly manifests whenever someone's lost, anywhere on Earth, even in Foundation sites, this definitely fits the classification for Keter. Super Keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was considering Keter, but I wasn't fully sure, so I went Euclid as that's the safest bet. If enough people think it should be Keter, I'll update its classification.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I very much like it, but shouldn't it be Keter as it is essentially uncontainable? Even with SRA in important locations, one of these could feasibly show up ANYWHERE unprotected in ANY foundation site

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Saw this early on in the drafts forum and I like how it turned out. you get a +1 from me :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A little similar to the infinite ikea but different and interesting enough for a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Amazing scp,definitely one of my favorites you have my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here it is! My first SCP. I got a decent amount of critique, and I think it is suitable for the main site.

This was inspired by the all-too-common feeling of getting lost, and not recognising the street you're currently driving through. A street that you swear didn't exist before. Or maybe a hallway that you never noticed.

Image source is from here
